description:
Create a Talon voice module for e-discovery email triage.

Workspace:
- Talon User Directory: C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\ediscovery\
- Data Directory: C:\workspace\enron_data\

Implement a Python backend (ediscovery.py) and a Talon command file (ediscovery.talon) to parse, log, and organize raw .eml files.

The ediscovery.py file must define a Talon Module 'user.ediscovery' and a list 'user.ediscovery_category' containing "privileged", "responsive", "irrelevant", and "needs review". It must implement the following actions:
- user.ediscovery_tag(email_id: str, category: str): Extracts headers (Message-ID, Date, From, To, Subject) from the .eml file in the data directory, appends the metadata and category to C:\workspace\enron_data\triage_log.csv (including headers if the file is new), and moves the source file to C:\workspace\enron_data\categorized\{category}\{email_id}.eml.
- user.ediscovery_extract(email_id: str): Parses the .eml file and saves all decoded attachments to C:\workspace\enron_data\attachments\ using the naming convention {email_id}_{filename}.

The ediscovery.talon file must map the voice command "triage tag {user.ediscovery_category} <user.text>" to the tagging action and "triage extract <user.text>" to the extraction action.
